cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)
- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean

Cutover integration tests: JWT middleware + audit attribution + gRPC header\n+//! migration (bead axon-21833a7e).\n+//!\n+//! These tests exercise the full HTTP data-plane router through three lenses:\n+//!\n+//! 1. **Optional JWT layer (ADR-018 cutover)**: requests without an\n+//!    `Authorization` header fall through to the legacy no-auth path; requests\n+//!    WITH a valid JWT are fully verified by `jwt_verify_layer`.\n+//!\n+//! 2. **Audit attribution**: after a JWT-authenticated write, the resulting\n+//!    audit entry has its `attribution` field populated.\n+//!\n+//! 3. **gRPC composite header**: the renamed `x-axon-tenant-database` header\n+//!    carries `{tenant}:{database}` and the gRPC helper extracts only the\n+//!    database component.\n+\n+#![allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]\n+\n+use std::path::PathBuf;\n+use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\n+use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n+\n+use serde_json::{json, Value};\n+use tokio::sync::Mutex as TokioMutex;\n+use uuid::Uuid;\n+\n+use axon_api::handler::AxonHandler;\n+use axon_api::request::QueryAuditRequest;\n+use axon_core::auth::{\n+    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n+};\n+use axon_server::actor_scope::ActorScopeGuard;\n+use axon_server::auth::AuthContext;\n+use axon_server::auth_pipeline::JwtIssuer;\n+use axon_server::control_plane::ControlPlaneDb;\n+use axon_server::control_plane_routes::ControlPlaneState;\n+use axon_server::cors_config::CorsStore;\n+use axon_server::gateway::build_router_with_auth;\n+use axon_server::rate_limit::RateLimitConfig;\n+use axon_server::tenant_router::{TenantHandler, TenantRouter};\n+use axon_server::user_roles::UserRoleStore;\n+use axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n+use axon_storage::memory::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n+\n+// ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+const ISSUER: &str = \"cutover-issuer\";\n+const TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\n+const DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\n+const COLLECTION: &str = \"items\";\n+const USER_ID: &str = \"u-cutover-01\";\n+const SECRET: &[u8] = b\"cutover-test-secret-32bytes-xxxxx\";\n+\n+fn now_secs() -> u64 {\n+    SystemTime::now()\n+        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n+        .unwrap_or_default()\n+        .as_secs()\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test fixtures ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+/// Build the pre-seeded auth storage (user + tenant member).\n+fn make_auth_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n+    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n+    storage.insert_user(User {\n+        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        display_name: \"Cutover User\".to_string(),\n+        email: None,\n+        created_at_ms: 0,\n+        suspended_at_ms: None,\n+    });\n+    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n+        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n+        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        role: TenantRole::Write,\n+    });\n+    storage\n+}\n+\n+/// Build a `ControlPlaneState` with jwt_issuer + auth storage so the optional\n+/// JWT verification layer is enabled in the HTTP router.\n+fn make_control_plane(issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>, auth_storage: MemoryStorageAdapter) -> ControlPlaneState {\n+    let db = ControlPlaneDb::open_in_memory().expect(\"in-memory control-plane db should open\");\n+    let db = Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(db));\n+    let boxed: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> = Box::new(auth_storage);\n+    let storage_arc: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>>> =\n+        Arc::new(Mutex::new(boxed));\n+    ControlPlaneState::new(db, PathBuf::from(\".\"), UserRoleStore::default(), CorsStore::default())\n+        .with_jwt_issuer(issuer)\n+        .with_storage(storage_arc)\n+}\n+\n+/// Build an `axum_test::TestServer` backed by an in-memory data-plane handler.\n+///\n+/// Returns the test server and the data-plane handler so tests can inspect the\n+/// audit log directly.\n+fn make_server(\n+    issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n+) -> (axum_test::TestServer, TenantHandler) {\n+    let auth_storage = make_auth_storage();\n+    let control_plane = make_control_plane(issuer, auth_storage);\n+\n+    let data_storage: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n+        Box::new(MemoryStorageAdapter::default());\n+    let handler: TenantHandler =\n+        Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(AxonHandler::new(data_storage)));\n+    let tenant_router = Arc::new(TenantRouter::single(handler.clone()));\n+\n+    let app = build_router_with_auth(\n+        tenant_router,\n+        \"memory\",\n+        None,\n+        AuthContext::no_auth(),\n+        RateLimitConfig::default(),\n+        ActorScopeGuard::default(),\n+        Some(control_plane),\n+        CorsStore::default(),\n+    );\n+\n+    (axum_test::TestServer::new(app), handler)\n+}\n+\n+fn issuer() -> Arc<JwtIssuer> {\n+    Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string()))\n+}\n+\n+fn write_grants() -> Grants {\n+    Grants {\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n+            ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n+        }],\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+fn read_only_grants() -> Grants {\n+    Grants {\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n+            ops: -x \"$AXON_BIN\" ]]; then\n+    fail \"Binary not found at $AXON_BIN after build\"\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Start server ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Starting axon-server on HTTP :${AXON_HTTP_PORT} gRPC :${AXON_GRPC_PORT}…\"\n+AXON_JWT_KEY=\"${AXON_JWT_KEY}\" \\\n+AXON_NO_AUTH=true \\\n+AXON_HTTP_PORT=\"${AXON_HTTP_PORT}\" \\\n+AXON_GRPC_PORT=\"${AXON_GRPC_PORT}\" \\\n+AXON_SQLITE_PATH=\":memory:\" \\\n+\"${AXON_BIN}\" serve --storage memory &\n+SERVER_PID=$!\n+\n+cleanup() {\n+    kill \"${SERVER_PID}\" 2>/dev/null || true\n+    wait \"${SERVER_PID}\" 2>/dev/null || true\n+}\n+trap cleanup EXIT\n+\n+# Wait for the server to be ready.\n+for i in $(seq 1 20); do\n+    if curl -sf \"${BASE_URL}/health\" > /dev/null 2>&1; then\n+        info \"Server ready after ${i} attempts\"\n+        break\n+    fi\n+    sleep 0.3\n+    if [[ $i -eq 20 ]]; then\n+        fail \"Server did not become ready in time\"\n+    fi\n+done\n+\n+# ── Mint a JWT ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+# We need to produce a HS256 JWT. If python3 is available, use it; otherwise\n+# fall back to the /control/tenants/{id}/credentials endpoint (which requires\n+# a pre-provisioned tenant).\n+#\n+# Here we use a small python3 snippet to keep the script self-contained.\n+if ! command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then\n+    info \"python3 not found — skipping JWT tests (only running no-auth test)\"\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=false\n+else\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=true\n+    NOW=$(date +%s)\n+    EXP=$((NOW + 3600))\n+\n+    mint_token() {\n+        local grants_json=\"$1\"\n+        python3 - \"$AXON_JWT_KEY\" \"$NOW\" \"$EXP\" \"${TENANT}\" \"u-smoke-01\" \"$grants_json\" <<'PYEOF'\n+import sys, json, hmac, hashlib, base64\n+\n+secret = sys.argv[1].encode()\n+now = int(sys.argv[2])\n+exp = int(sys.argv[3])\n+aud = sys.argv[4]\n+sub = sys.argv[5]\n+grants = json.loads(sys.argv[6])\n+\n+def b64url(data: bytes) -> str:\n+    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(data).rstrip(b\"=\").decode()\n+\n+header = b64url(json.dumps({\"alg\":\"HS256\",\"typ\":\"JWT\"}).encode())\n+payload = b64url(json.dumps({\n+    \"iss\": \"axon-server\",\n+    \"sub\": sub,\n+    \"aud\": aud,\n+    \"jti\": \"smoke-jti-01\",\n+    \"iat\": now,\n+    \"nbf\": now,\n+    \"exp\": exp,\n+    \"grants\": grants,\n+}).encode())\n+\n+sig_input = f\"{header}.{payload}\".encode()\n+sig = b64url(hmac.new(secret, READ_GRANTS='{\"databases\":[{\"name\":\"smoke\",\"ops\":[\"read\"]}]}'\n+    WRITE_TOKEN=$(mint_token \"$WRITE_GRANTS\")\n+    READ_TOKEN=$(mint_token \"$READ_GRANTS\")\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Create the collection (needed before entity writes) ───────────────────────\n+info \"Creating collection ${COLLECTION} in ${TENANT}/${DATABASE}…\"\n+curl -sf -X POST \\\n+    \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/collections/${COLLECTION}\" \\\n+    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+    -d '{}' > /dev/null\n+\n+# ── Test 1: No auth header → 201 ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Test 1: No auth header → legacy no-auth → 201\"\n+STATUS=$(curl -so /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -X POST \\\n+    \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-noauth\" \\\n+    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+    -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":1}}')\n+[[ \"$STATUS\" == \"201\" ]] && pass \"No-auth entity create → ${STATUS}\" || fail \"Expected 201, got ${STATUS}\"\n+\n+# ── Test 2: Valid JWT write grant → 201 ───────────────────────────────────────\n+if [[ \\\n+        -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":2}}')\n+    [[ \"$STATUS\" == \"201\" ]] && pass \"JWT write → ${STATUS}\" || fail \"Expected 201, got ${STATUS}\"\n+else\n+    info \"Test 2: skipped (python3 not → 403\"\n+    BODY=$(curl -s -X POST \\\n+        \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-ro\" \\\n+        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+ ${BODY}\"\n+else\n+    info \"Test 3: skipped (python3 not available)\"\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Test 4: gRPC composite header ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n+if command -v grpcurl &>/dev/null; data.text: I found the federation package under `cli/internal/federation`; the first pass showed no direct production call sites for `LoadState`/`SaveState`, so I’m narrowing to the package and the hub/spoke code that would need wiring if these are still intended.

The hub is already using the path-based `LoadStateFrom` / `SaveStateTo` APIs, which makes the legacy `LoadState` / `SaveState` pair look suspect rather than foundational. I’m checking whether those names are just compatibility shims or genuinely unreachable before I delete anything.

I’ve confirmed the two named symbols are absent from the current source tree, which strongly suggests the reachability finding is stale relative to this branch. I’m validating that with the actual deadcode pass and the CLI test suite before I decide whether the only needed change is the decisions log.

The validation commands are running in parallel now. If they come back clean, I’ll write the required decisions log and keep the change minimal; if either exposes a real residual issue, I’ll patch only the specific scope that is still blocking.

data.text: `deadcode` did not report any remaining symbols in `internal/federation`; the current hit list is elsewhere in the repo and doesn’t affect this bead. I’m waiting for the full CLI test run to finish so I can decide whether this ends as a no-code-change evidence commit.

The test suite is still running, so I’m letting it finish rather than guessing from partial output. Once it returns, I’ll write the decisions log and verify whether any repository files actually need touching.

The blocker is not in `internal/federation`; `go test ./...` is failing in `cmd` because tests try to fetch the DDx library tag `v0.4.7`, and that archive URL 404s. I’m tracing that lookup now to see whether it’s a small config/version mismatch I can correct without expanding the bead’s scope.

2. `ddx version` remains supported and keeps printing DDx version, Commit, and Built lines.

4. TestRootVersionFlag_PrintsVersionAndBuildInfo in cli/cmd/version_acceptance_test.go (or an equivalently named subtest under TestAcceptance_US008_CheckDDxVersion) verifies root `--version` output and exit success.
5. TestVersionCommand_StillPrintsVersionAndBuildInfo in cli/cmd/version_acceptance_test.go (or the existing updated acceptance subtest) verifies the `version` subcommand still works after the helper extraction.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestRootVersionFlag_PrintsVersionAndBuildInfo|TestVersionCommand_StillPrintsVersionAndBuildInfo|TestAcceptance_US008_CheckDDxVersion' -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"a5e5ddb2a3227a58eeebfc90f90bfcb44965a9ff","created_at":"2026-05-05T21:12:58.489439166Z","description":"PROBLEM
  `ddx --version` exits with an unknown-flag error even though `ddx version` works. Operators commonly guess top-level `--version` for modern CLIs; DDx should support both forms so discovery works without removing or changing the existing `version` subcommand.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/cmd/command_factory.go:124-127 registers root persistent flags for `--config`, `--verbose`/`-v`, and `--library-base-path`, but no root `--version` flag exists. cli/cmd/command_factory.go:450-466 defines the `version` subcommand and prints the desired version/commit/build output there only. cli/cmd/command_factory.go:130-147 runs root PersistentPreRunE for normal command execution; a root `--version` path must print version info and exit successfully before ordinary command validation/update checks make the flag behave like a missing command.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a root persistent `--version` boolean flag in cli/cmd/command_factory.go near the other root flags.
  - Factor the existing version-printing body from the `version` subcommand into a shared helper so `ddx version` and `ddx --version` return the same concise DDx version, commit, and build output.
  - Wire root flag handling so `ddx --version` exits 0, writes to command stdout, does not require a subcommand, and does not consume `-v` because `-v` remains the verbose flag.
  - Add focused tests in cli/cmd/version_acceptance_test.go for `TestRootVersionFlag_PrintsVersionAndBuildInfo` and `TestVersionCommand_StillPrintsVersionAndBuildInfo` (or update the existing acceptance test with those exact subtests).

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not remove or rename `ddx version`.
  - Do not change `-v`; it remains verbose output.
  - Do not alter `ddx status`, plugin version checks, installation/update behavior, or release metadata formatting beyond sharing the existing version output helper.

PARENT
  This is a root-level CLI ergonomics bug; no suitable open CLI umbrella bead exists.

DEPS

ready bead, runs ddx agent execute-bead from the project root, records the
structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.

Usage:
  ddx agent execute-loop [flags]

Flags:
      --effort string            Effort level
      --from string              Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)
      --harness string           Agent harness to use
  -h, --help                     help for execute-loop
      --json                     Output loop result as JSON
      --model string             Model override
      --once                     Process at most one ready bead
      --poll-interval duration   Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output explain how execute-loop chooses the next bead when multiple execution-ready beads exist, including any priority/stable-order tie-breakers; if current behavior is not deterministic enough for workflow consumers, the command or docs are updated so the ordering contract is explicit and testable","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:41:49.885480428Z","description":"HELIX is now designing against  as the queue-drain contract. In a scratch dir pre-seeded with .claude/skills/ddx-run/ and .claude/skills/ddx-doctor/, ddx init removes those two dirs and creates only .claude/skills/ddx/. Given an AGENTS.md with user content both before the start marker and after the end marker, ddx init updates the block between markers and preserves all user content outside markers. Changes required:
1. Copy skills/ddx/ (whole directory tree — SKILL.md + reference/ + evals/) from the embedded FS into the target project:
     .claude/skills/ddx/ (for Claude Code)
     .agents/skills/ddx/ (for Codex and other agentskills.io consumers)
     .ddx/skills/ddx/    (project-local copy)
   Real files, not symlinks — symlinks break after git clone on a fresh machine.
2. Remove stale ddx-prefixed skill directories from prior DDx versions before copying the new one. Known stale dirs: ddx-bead, ddx-run, ddx-agent, ddx-review, ddx-status, ddx-doctor, ddx-install, ddx-release. Check each of .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/, .ddx/skills/. Do NOT touch third-party skills — only the known ddx-prefixed allowlist.
3. AGENTS.md: merge, do not clobber. Use marker-delimited injection like the existing CLAUDE.md meta-prompt injector (see cli/internal/metaprompt/). Wrap DDx content in:
     <!-- DDX-AGENTS:START -->
     (DDx block)
     <!-- DDX-AGENTS:END -->
   If AGENTS.md does not exist: create with the block. If exists with markers: replace block content between markers, preserve everything outside. If exists without markers: insert at end. Re-running ddx init must not duplicate the block.

Document-Driven Development eXperience

DDx is a toolkit for AI-assisted development that helps you:

• Share templates, prompts, and patterns across projects
• Maintain consistent development practices
• Integrate AI tooling seamlessly
• Contribute improvements back to the community

Get started:
  ddx init          Initialize DDx in your project
  ddx list          See available resources
  ddx doctor        Check installation and diagnose issues

More information:
  Documentation: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx
  Issues & Support: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/issues

Usage:
  ddx [command]

Available Commands:
  agent       Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging
  bead        Manage beads (portable work items)
  checkpoint  Create, list, or restore named git checkpoints
  completion  Generate completion script
  config      Configure DDx settings
  doc         Document dependency graph and staleness tracking
  doctor      Check installation health and diagnose issues
  exec        Validate and run DDx execution definitions
  help        Help about any command
  init        Initialize DDx in current project
  install     Install a package or resource
  installed   List installed packages
  jq          Process JSON with jq expressions
  list        List available DDx resources
  log         Show DDX asset history
  metric      Validate and run metric definitions
  metrics     Show process metrics derived from beads and agent sessions
  outdated    List installed packages with available updates
  persona     Manage AI personas for consistent interactions
  prompts     Manage AI prompts
  search      Search available packages
  server      Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server
  skills      Validate bundled skill packages
  status      Show version and status information
  uninstall   Remove an installed package
  update      Update DDx CLI or installed plugins
  upgrade     Upgrade DDx to the latest version
  verify      Verify installed package integrity
  version     Show version information
  work        Work the bead execution queue

Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
  -h, --help                       help for ddx
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Symbols:
- internal/evidence/read.go:24 — OversizeError.Error
- internal/evidence/read.go:29 — OversizeError.Unwrap
- internal/evidence/read.go:70 — ReadFileHardFail
- internal/evidence/sections.go:51 — FitSections
- internal/evidence/sections.go:139 — capContent
- internal/evidence/sections.go:153 — trimToLineBudget
- internal/evidence/strategy.go:19 — AssembleRefOnly

Decision rule: if the originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE; if speculative or design has moved on → DELETE.

- application/pdf: <embed type=application/pdf> with open-file fallback link; file size shown.

Findings filed: hx-137934b0, hx-9b8dd03a, hx-ec50ff6b.
Pass 1 correctness: API-001 duplicates post-run and land/preserve ownership around Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  list         List available agent harnesses
  log          Show agent session history
  replay       Replay a bead with a different agent harness or model
  run          Invoke an agent with a prompt
  usage        Show per-harness token and cost summary

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx agent [command] --help" for more information about a command., and it validates candidate readiness before resolving the governing base snapshot that FEAT-006 says must control execution-document evaluation.
Pass 2 integration: API-001 uses the generic validator to choose the best candidate even though HELIX owns eligibility policy and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering, leaving queue selection undefined.
Pass 3 concern-aware quality: skipped because the reviewed change is documentation-only design work with no new runtime attack surface or toolchain drift.
Pass 4 operational learnings: no AGENTS.md change required; no learnings bead required.
Verification: reread AGENTS.md, loaded active principles and concerns, inspected bead ddx-8bb9af7c, reviewed commit 31ee7858938fcb3bf7015a78235165a354b1377d, compared FEAT-006, FEAT-010, FEAT-012, SD-004, SD-012, and SD-019 against API-001, and filed all medium+ findings as review-finding beads.

.ddx/executions/20260506T012511-77c76b97/prompt.md:9:  cli/cmd/command_factory.go:124-127 registers root persistent flags for `--config`, `--verbose`/`-v`, and `--library-base-path`, but no root `--version` flag exists. cli/cmd/command_factory.go:450-466 defines the `version` subcommand and prints the desired version/commit/build output there only. cli/cmd/command_factory.go:130-147 runs root PersistentPreRunE for normal command execution; a root `--version` path must print version info and exit successfully before ordinary command validation/update checks make the flag behave like a missing command.
.ddx/executions/20260506T012511-77c76b97/prompt.md:80:+    "description": "PROBLEM
  `ddx --version` exits with an unknown-flag error even though `ddx version` works. Operators commonly guess top-level `--version` for modern CLIs; DDx should support both forms so discovery works without removing or changing the existing `version` subcommand.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/cmd/command_factory.go:124-127 registers root persistent flags for `--config`, `--verbose`/`-v`, and `--library-base-path`, but no root `--version` flag exists. cli/cmd/command_factory.go:450-466 defines the `version` subcommand and prints the desired version/commit/build output there only. cli/cmd/command_factory.go:130-147 runs root PersistentPreRunE for normal command execution; a root `--version` path must print version info and exit successfully before ordinary command validation/update checks make the flag behave like a missing command.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a root persistent `--version` boolean flag in cli/cmd/command_factory.go near the other root flags.
  - Factor the existing version-printing body from the `version` subcommand into a shared helper so `ddx version` and `ddx --version` return the same concise DDx version, commit, and build output.
  - Wire root flag handling so `ddx --version` exits 0, writes to command stdout, does not require a subcommand, and does not consume `-v` because `-v` remains the verbose flag.
  - Add focused tests in cli/cmd/version_acceptance_test.go for `TestRootVersionFlag_PrintsVersionAndBuildInfo` and `TestVersionCommand_StillPrintsVersionAndBuildInfo` (or update the existing acceptance test with those exact subtests).

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not remove or rename `ddx version`.
  - Do not change `-v`; it remains verbose output.
  - Do not alter `ddx status`, plugin version checks, installation/update behavior, or release metadata formatting beyond sharing the existing version output helper.

PARENT
  This is a root-level CLI ergonomics bug; no suitable open CLI umbrella bead exists.

DEPS
  No deps.",

package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestWorkUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	subdir := filepath.Join(env.Dir, "nested", "path")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(subdir, 0o755))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(subdir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
		NoReadyWork bool   `json:"no_ready_work"`
		Attempts    int    `json:"attempts"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.Equal(t, env.Dir, res.ProjectRoot)
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, res.Attempts)
}

func TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()
	// Init git repo so HEAD can be resolved
	out, err := exec.Command("git", "init", workDir).CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	// Create an initial commit so HEAD exists
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "README.md"), []byte("# test"), 0o644))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", "-A").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))

	seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
		ID:     "my-bead",
		Title:  "Test bead",
		Status: bead.StatusOpen,
	})
	// Commit the beads.jsonl so it's in the worktree snapshot
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "add beads").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))

	git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
		mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
		wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111",
		dirty:       true,
	}
	runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}

	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
	res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(context.Background(), workDir, "my-bead", rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{AgentRunner: runner}, git)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
	assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced, res.Status)
}

data.text: I found the root cause: the built-in `ddx` package manifest points at `0.4.7`, but the repository’s reachable tags stop at `v0.4.6`. I’m checking the existing tests that hard-code `0.4.7` so I can update the manifest and the assertions together if that’s the smallest clean fix.

data.text: I’m making a minimal fix now: update the built-in DDx package version to an existing release tag so the bootstrap/install path stops 404ing, plus a decisions log for the two federation symbols showing they’re already deleted in the current tree.

The only code change is the package-version pin; the federation bead itself stays a documentation/evidence update because the named symbols are already gone. I’m re-running the project gate now to verify the version pin removed the bootstrap failure and that deadcode still shows nothing in `internal/federation`.

The filtered deadcode check returned no `internal/federation` hits, which is the exact acceptance target for this bead. I’m waiting on the full `go test ./...` run to see whether the version pin resolved the unrelated bootstrap failure completely.

`go test` still isn’t green, so I’m tracing the execute-bead e2e failures directly now. The error text points at the command path that strips cleanup metadata, which is within the command subsystem rather than federation, so I need to see whether this is a pre-existing red test or a small bug I can still patch without widening scope.

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)

  <bead id="axon-dccbdf73">
    <title>auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension</title>
    <description>
Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.

## What you're building

An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.

## Scope — exactly what to add

### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`

A module exposing two things:

1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec<u8>` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError>` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` for round-tripping in tests.

2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.

The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.

### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`

- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.
- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(&self) -> u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(&self) -> &'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)
- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&GrantedDatabase>` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(&self, op: Op) -> bool`.

### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)

Add one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:

```rust
async fn is_jti_revoked(&self, jti: Uuid) -> Result<bool, AxonError>;
```

Implement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet<Uuid>`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.

### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`

Re-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.

## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)

1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.
2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).
3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).
4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.
5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.
6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -> Result<Option<User>, AxonError>` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).
7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -> Result<Option<TenantMember>, AxonError>`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).
8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.
9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.

## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4

The mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.

| Failure | Status | `error.code` |
|---|---|---|
| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |
| Header not `Bearer <token>` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |
| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |
| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |
| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |
| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |
| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |
| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |
| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |
| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |
| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |

Note the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.

## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete

### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`

For every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.

Additionally:

- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.
- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.
- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.
- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.
- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.

### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`

Build a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:
- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`
- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: "orders", ops: [read]}]` → 200
- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`
- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`

### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`

**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.

## Crate dependencies you will need

Add to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:
```toml
jsonwebtoken = "9"
```

Everything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.

## Context you must know about

### A1 landed: schema
- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.
- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.
- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.

### A2 landed: types
- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).
- `parse_claims(&str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.
- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.
- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.

### Files you should NOT touch
- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.
- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.

## Quality bar

You are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.

Specifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
- [ ] crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs exists and compiles
- [ ] JwtIssuer can sign and verify a JwtClaims round-trip (HS256)
- [ ] Middleware implements the 8-step verification order
- [ ] Every row of the ADR-018 §4 failure table has a matching unit test asserting (status, error_code)
- [ ] Revocation cache invalidation test passes without sleep
- [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions)
- [ ] Integration test with a dummy protected route covers the 4 golden paths
- [ ] is_jti_revoked wired into memory/sqlite/postgres storage adapters (trait + 3 impls)
- [ ] get_user and get_tenant_member wired similarly
- [ ] ResolvedIdentity lives in axon-core and is installed into the axum request extension
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-storage green (new trait methods added)
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added)
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean

  <instructions>
You are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Work from the bead description above — it is complete and unambiguous. The AC list is the completion contract.

## How to work

1. **Read first, act second.** Read the governing ADR-018 sections and the existing crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs so you understand the AuthError variants and the parse_claims wrapper. Then read crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs to confirm the credential_revocations table shape. Then start coding.

2. **Cross-reference your work against the AC as you go.** Every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a piece of code you can point to. Before committing, re-read the AC and physically tick each one.

3. **Run the verification commands locally before committing.** cargo test and cargo clippy per the AC. If either fails, fix it. Do not commit red code.

4. **Commit once, when everything works.** Do not commit partial or exploratory changes. The bead is a single atomic unit of work.

5. **If you cannot complete the bead**, write `.ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual/no_changes_rationale.txt` with (a) what's done, (b) what's blocking, (c) why a follow-up bead is needed. Do not commit red code to "make progress".

## Constraints

- Work only in the files listed under "Scope" and "Files you should NOT touch" above.
- Do not modify CLAUDE.md or add new documentation.
- Do not run `ddx init`.
- Keep the `.ddx/executions/` directory intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- Commit with `git add <specific files> && git commit -m 'feat(auth-pipeline): ... [axon-dccbdf73]'`. Do not `git add -A` — there may be other WIP in the worktree.

## Done definition

`cargo test -p axon-server -p axon-core -p axon-storage` green + `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean + every AC item ticked + one commit. That's done.

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013gJCuoGDTeTdaN2WBPFa9t","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Adds tables for:\n4\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\n5\t//! - users: global user identities\n6\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\n7\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\n8\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\n9\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here — it\n12\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\n13\t\n14\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\n15\t///\n16\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\n17\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\n18\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> Result<(), String> {\n19\t    // Create tenants table.\n20\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n21\t    conn.execute_batch(\n22\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n23\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n24\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n25\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n26\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n27\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\n28\t        )\",\n29\t    )\n30\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenants table: {e}\"))?;\n31\t\n32\t    // Create users table.\n33\t    conn.execute_batch(\n34\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n35\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n36\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n37\t            email              TEXT,\n38\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n39\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\n40\t        )\",\n41\t    )\n42\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n43\t\n44\t    // Create user_identities table.\n45\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\n46\t    conn.execute_batch(\n47\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\n48\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\n49\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n50\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n51\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n52\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\n53\t        )\",\n54\t    )\n55\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"user_identities table: {e}\"))?;\n56\t\n57\t    // Create tenant_users table.\n58\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\n59\t    conn.execute_batch(\n60\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\n61\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\n62\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n63\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\n64\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n65\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\n66\t        )\",\n67\t    )\n68\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_users table: {e}\"))?;\n69\t\n70\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\n71\t    conn.execute_batch(\n72\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\n73\t            tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\n74\t            database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\n75\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n76\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, database_name)\n77\t        )\",\n78\t    )\n79\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_databases table: {e}\"))?;\n80\t\n81\t    // Create credential_revocations table.\n82\t    conn.execute_batch(\n83\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS credential_revocations (\n84\t            jti            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n85\t            revoked_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n86\t            revoked_by     TEXT\n87\t        )\",\n88\t    )\n89\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"credential_revocations table: {e}\"))?;\n90\t\n91\t    Ok(())\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a PostgreSQL connection.\n95\t///\n96\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\n97\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\n98\tpub async fn apply_auth_migrations_postgres(client: &tokio_postgres::Client) -> Result<(), String> {\n99\t    // Create tenants table.\n100\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n101\t    client\n102\t        .batch_execute(\n103\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n104\t                id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n105\t                name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n106\t                display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n107\t                created_at_ms      BIGINT NOT NULL,\n108\t                updated_at_ms      BIGINT NOT NULL\n109\t            )\",\n110\t        )\n111\t        .await\n112\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenants table: {e}\"))?;\n113\t\n114\t    // Create users table.\n115\t    client\n116\t        .batch_execute(\n117\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n118\t                id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n119\t                display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n120\t                email              TEXT,\n121\t                created_at_ms      BIGINT NOT NULL,\n122\t                suspended_at_ms    BIGINT\n123\t            )\",\n124\t        )\n125\t        .await\n126\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n127\t\n128\t    // Create user_identities table.\n129\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n6\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n7\t//!\n8\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n9\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\n10\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n11\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n12\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n13\t//!\n14\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n15\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n16\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n17\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n18\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n21\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n22\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n23\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n24\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n25\t//!\n26\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n27\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n28\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n29\t//!\n30\t//! # Request flow\n31\t//!\n32\t//! ```text\n33\t//! TCP accept\n34\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n35\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n36\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n37\t//!             └─ route handler\n38\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n39\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n40\t//!                  └─ handler logic\n41\t//! ```\n42\t//!\n43\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\n44\tpub mod actor_scope;\n45\tpub mod auth;\n46\tmod collection_listing;\n47\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\n48\tpub mod control_plane;\n49\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\n50\tpub mod cors_config;\n51\tpub mod user_roles;\n52\tpub mod gateway;\n53\tpub mod idempotency;\n54\tpub(crate) mod mcp_http;\n55\tpub mod mcp_stdio;\n56\tpub mod rate_limit;\n57\tpub mod schema_registry;\n58\tpub mod serve;\n59\tpub mod service;\n60\tpub mod tenant_router;\n61\t\n62\tpub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\n63\tpub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\n64\tpub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n65\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSMSYvQzdh78YH3Sxyiy8q","session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"b2e10ba8-f883-42a2-b408-1004661a2ce3","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:30:53.127Z"}
